raw_text:
Director of Platform Engineering

CloudOps Dynamics — Building the infrastructure that scales tomorrow's applications

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$280,000 - $420,000 base + equity and performance bonus

The Opportunity

We are seeking an experienced Director of Platform Engineering to lead our Platform Engineering

organization and drive the strategic evolution of our internal developer platform and infrastructure. In

this role, you will lead a team of 12-15 engineers across multiple specialties, set technical vision and

strategy, and partner with product and operations leadership to shape how our customers build and

deploy applications. You'll be responsible for scaling our platform capabilities, mentoring senior

engineers, and establishing best practices across the organization. This is a hands-on leadership role

requiring both strategic thinking and technical depth.

About CloudOps Dynamics

CloudOps Dynamics is a mid-stage SaaS infrastructure company focused on delivering enterprise-
grade platform engineering solutions. We help organizations modernize their development

infrastructure, improve developer velocity, and reduce operational overhead through intelligent
platform abstractions and tooling.

Day-to-Day

Lead, mentor, and develop a growing team of platform engineers including senior engineers, staff

engineers, and managers, conducting performance reviews and creating career development paths

Define and execute the multi-year technical strategy for the internal developer platform, Kubernetes
infrastructure, and infrastructure-as-code practices across the organization

Establish platform engineering standards and governance; architect solutions using Kubernetes,
Terraform, Backstage, and custom tooling to improve developer experience and reduce time-to-
market

Partner with VP of Engineering and product leadership on quarterly roadmap planning, resource
allocation, and cross-functional initiatives that affect platform architecture

Build and maintain relationships with key enterprise customers, presenting technical roadmap
items and gathering requirements for platform capabilities

Design and implement observability, monitoring, and cost optimization strategies across the
Kubernetes clusters and infrastructure footprint

Lead architectural decisions around Go and Python service standardization, deployment pipelines,
and platform abstractions

Champion a culture of innovation and operational excellence; establish metrics, SLOs, and KPIs to

measure platform performance and team productivity

 Drive recruitment, retention, and technical onboarding of high-caliber engineering talent to scale

the platform engineering organization

You Have

10+ years of software engineering experience with at least 5+ years leading and managing

engineering teams (including managing managers)

5+ years of hands-on experience with Kubernetes administration, architecture, and cluster

management in production environments

3+ years of experience with infrastructure-as-code tooling (primarily Terraform) and GitOps

practices

Proven track record designing and scaling internal developer platforms or developer experience

infrastructure at mid-to-large scale

Deep expertise in Go and/or Python for platform tooling, automation, and service development

Strong understanding of observability, monitoring, logging, and tracing (Prometheus, ELK, Jaeger,

or similar)

Bonus

Experience with Backstage or similar developer portal and API discovery systems

Hands-on experience managing multi-cloud or hybrid infrastructure strategies

Prior experience at a venture-backed infrastructure or developer tools company

Familiarity with eBPF and advanced Linux networking for platform observability

Our Stack

Kubernetes   Terraform   Backstage   Go   Python   Prometheus   Helm   ArgoCD   Vault   Envoy

What We Offer

Competitive equity package (stock options with 4-year vest and 1-year cliff)

Comprehensive health coverage including medical, dental, and vision for employee and

dependents

401(k) matching up to 5% and financial planning support

Unlimited PTO and 12 paid company holidays

Professional development budget ($5,000/year) for conferences, courses, and certifications

Home office setup stipend ($2,500) and monthly internet reimbursement

Quarterly all-hands gatherings in San Francisco (travel covered)

Apply

Apply directly through our careers page at cloudopsdynamics.com/careers or send your resume and a
brief cover letter describing your experience leading platform engineering organizations to
careers@cloudopsdynamics.com. Include links to GitHub or technical projects where relevant. We

review applications on a rolling basis.
nice_to_have: Experience with Backstage or similar developer portal and API discovery systems
Hands-on experience managing multi-cloud or hybrid infrastructure strategies
Prior experience at a venture-backed infrastructure or developer tools company
Familiarity with eBPF and advanced Linux networking for platform observability
